name: zig-docs
description: Fetches Zig language and standard library documentation via CLI. Activates when needing Zig API details, std lib function signatures, or language reference content that isn't covered in zig-best-practices.
capabilities: Fetch Zig standard library source with doc comments from GitHub
Retrieve language reference content from ziglang.org using pandoc
List all std lib modules via GitHub API
Extract function documentation and signatures from Zig source
Access version-specific documentation for stable releases
author: 0xBigBoss
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation/guide skill containing only markdown instructions. No executable code, no network requests, no filesystem access, and no external command execution. All static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown syntax delimiters, URLs are legitimate Zig documentation sources, and JSON field patterns are incorrectly flagged. The skill only provides reference guidance for fetching Zig documentation.
valueStatement: Need to look up Zig API details or standard library function signatures. This skill provides curl and pandoc commands to fetch Zig language and standard library documentation from authoritative GitHub sources.
limitations: Does not provide interactive documentation search within the terminal
Requires curl, pandoc, and jq installed on the system
HTML documentation at ziglang.org is JavaScript-rendered and cannot be fetched directly

useCases.title: Look up std lib API
useCases.description: Quickly fetch function signatures and documentation for std lib modules like mem, fs, or log.

useCases.title: Read language reference
useCases.description: Access the Zig language reference and beginner guide for learning the language syntax and concepts.

useCases.title: Get Zig help
useCases.description: Enable Claude to fetch authoritative Zig documentation when writing or reviewing Zig code.

promptTemplates.title: Fetch std lib module
promptTemplates.scenario: Need documentation for a specific module
promptTemplates.prompt: Get the documentation for the Zig std lib [module_name] module, including function signatures and doc comments.

promptTemplates.title: Look up function
promptTemplates.scenario: Need details about a specific function
promptTemplates.prompt: Find documentation for [function_name] in the Zig standard library and show its signature and usage.

promptTemplates.title: Language reference
promptTemplates.prompt: Fetch the Zig language reference section about [topic] and explain the key concepts.

promptTemplates.title: Version-specific docs
promptTemplates.scenario: Need docs for a specific Zig version
promptTemplates.prompt: Get the Zig [version] documentation for the [module/function] from the official release.

bestPractices: Use raw.githubusercontent.com for std lib source - most reliable with doc comments
Specify version tags like 0.14.0 for stable documentation across sessions
Pipe to grep and head for quick lookups instead of fetching full files
antiPatterns: Avoid fetching JavaScript-rendered HTML docs from ziglang.org/std - they return empty content
Do not rely on GitHub API for raw content - use raw.githubusercontent.com to avoid rate limits
Do not fetch full language reference without piping to head - it is approximately 500KB of text

faq.question: Which documentation source is most reliable?
faq.answer: Raw GitHub source files from raw.githubusercontent.com are most reliable and include doc comments.

faq.question: What tools are required?
faq.answer: This skill requires curl, pandoc, and jq installed on your system to fetch and parse documentation.

faq.question: Can I get version-specific docs?
faq.answer: Yes. Replace master with a version tag like 0.14.0 in URLs for stable release documentation.

faq.question: Does this skill store any data?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides instructions for fetching documentation. No data is stored or cached.

faq.question: Why is ziglang.org documentation empty?
faq.answer: The std lib HTML docs at ziglang.org are JavaScript-rendered. Use raw GitHub source instead.

faq.question: How is this different from zig-best-practices?
faq.answer: zig-best-practices covers coding patterns and idioms. zig-docs provides raw documentation lookup.
